title: Consolidate release identity and freeze timing
outOfScope: Creating or publishing a Git tag or provider release
Changing the public release archive format or installer behavior
Implementing unrelated plan Work Items 2 through 11

sources.reason: Provider workflow source resolution and publication ordering.
Freeze finalizer currently writes metadata that can change HEAD and uses symbolic HEAD evidence.
Release preflight must enforce one immutable source commit and one lifecycle timing model.
Regression coverage for stale source, archive, lifecycle, and candidate/published identity failures.
Canonical state and projection identity constraints.
English release procedure and evidence semantics.
Japanese release procedure and evidence semantics.
Release finalization entry points and their ordering.
Functional correction budget and hard archive-growth gate.
User-authorized serial remediation plan and publication stop rule.

rawUserRequest: 修复发布流程的根因：统一发布身份和冻结时序；发现流程缺陷时停止发布、优先改流程；预算、格式、Contract、Summary 问题纳入当前功能工单；完成当前工单的完整 Contract/Preflight/Finish/archive/commit/PR/merge/ai-close/base-sync 流程后再继续。

declaredIntent.summary: 在不发布版本的前提下，消除 release sourceCommit、tag target、metadata commit、candidate/published projection 和 freeze finalization 之间的多重权威与自引用，使昂贵发布检查前能一次性验证完整身份链。

riskAssessment.reason: The correction changes release gating and provider workflow ordering; it must remain fail closed and must not publish in this Work Item.

executionDecision.reason: The user explicitly authorized root-cause workflow correction and serial execution; publication remains out of scope.

preReviewWarnings: Do not use HEAD as a mutable release evidence identity after metadata writes.
Do not mix candidate-prepared, premerge-finalized, and post-close-finalized states in one release attempt.
Run low-cost identity, format, budget, Contract, Summary, and release preflight checks before provider CI or publication.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: A candidate freeze retains one explicit source commit while generated metadata changes do not retarget it.
The workflow resolves the merged default-branch source once, verifies projections, then tags and publishes without a second mutable freeze.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record checkpoints before implementation and Finish to prevent lifecycle drift.

acceptance: One explicit release identity tuple (sourceCommit, tagTarget, metadataCommit, releaseTag) is recorded and validated; mutable HEAD and self-referential metadata are rejected.

Release preflight rejects stale or symbolic source evidence, mismatched freeze/archive/projection digests, mixed lifecycle timing, and candidate/published identity conflicts before expensive provider checks.

The release workflow and Make targets expose one ordered sequence: merge and synchronize default branch, resolve source once, run low-cost preflight, generate source-bound evidence, then tag/provider release; this Work Item never publishes.
Regression tests cover source identity drift, archive/metadata commit drift, candidate versus published projections, and the accepted source-bound finalized candidate path.
English and Japanese distribution documentation describe the same state machine and distinguish candidate evidence, published evidence, and immutable source identity.
Budget, formatting, Contract, Summary, checkpoint, and status gates are part of this functional correction; no separate budget-only Work Item is created.
guidelines: Release metadata must have one canonical source of truth and explicit projection semantics.
Low-cost fail-closed checks must run before expensive provider CI or publication.
Template workflow rules must distinguish the template repository from adopter repositories.
Generated evidence must be committed before PR validation and must not be hand-edited after generation.

intent.problem: The release attempt repeatedly discovered adjacent process defects after expensive tests and PR cycles: source identity moved between HEAD, metadata commits, freeze archives, and provider release; candidate and published projections were conflated; and finalization occurred at incompatible lifecycle points.
constraints: Do not publish a version or create a tag in this Work Item.
Keep candidate and published evidence separate and source-bound.
Distinguish template and adopter repository boundaries.
intent.rationale: A single explicit identity tuple and one freeze-to-publish sequence prevents self-referential metadata and stops low-value full CI/PR loops before they begin.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User explicitly authorized root-cause process correction in release gates, CI, governance policy, and documentation.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore the prior release gates and documentation; immutable archived evidence is not rewritten.

repaymentRecords.reason: Consolidated functional release-identity/timing root correction; no budget-only Work Item. Repay during final plan cleanup after evidence is preserved.
